articles.title: Sebi issues notice to Capital Group FPIs in Ketan Parekh front-running case: Report
articles.description: The report said this is the first time Capital Group has been identified in a Sebi notice linked to the front-running case, after previously being referred to only as a ‘big client.’

articles.title: Secrets revealed in SpaceX's IPO filing: From Elon Musk's pay structure to Anthropic deal
articles.description: The filing showed SpaceX posted a loss of $4.9 billion in 2025 on revenue of $18.7 billion. Losses accelerated further in the first quarter of this year, with the company reporting a $4.3 billion loss on revenue of $4.7 billion.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia, Türkiye and the UAE are pulling the GCC Apart
articles.description: The danger for the GCC economies, and indeed the global economy, is that fragmentation and chronic instability become structural rather than episodic

articles.title: Rupee rises 41 paise from all-time closing low to 96.45 against US dollar
articles.description: The rupee recovered 41 paise from its all-time closing low to 96.45 against US dollar in early trade on Thursday, after US President Donald Trump indicated that negotiations with Iran were entering the final stages. Investors are still gauging the geopolitical risk and oil price sensitivity in the background with any flare-up in the Middle East tensions or spikes in crude, forex traders said. At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee opened at 96.25 against the US dollar, then touched 96.45 in early trade, up 41 paise from its all-time lifetime low of 96.86 against the US dollar on Wednesday. On Wednesday, the rupee made a new low of 96.95 and later closed at 96.86 which was also a new closing low as demand for dollars continued with dollar index higher and crude oil prices remaining on the higher side near to USD 110 during the day. "From a technical perspective, the 97.00 zone is expected to act as an immediate resistance area for USDINR, while support may emerge around

articles.title: Aurionpro wins 3-year deal with US based fintech platforms
articles.description: Aurionpro Solutions, through its US-based subsidiary Aurionpro Fintech Inc., announced its largest order win in the US market with the signing of a strategic three-year engagement with one of the leading fintech platforms in the United States specializing in digital insurance payments. The engagement marks a significant expansion of Aurionpro's existing relationship with the customer, which currently leverages our cutting-edge payments framework software and allied services.

articles.title: 300 more families to benefit from ViswaSanthi Foundation’s latest water plant in Kuttanad
articles.description: ViswaSanthi Foundation marks Mohanlal’s 66th birthday with a major clean water initiative in Kuttanad, Kerala, addressing local drinking water scarcity.

articles.description: Warren Buffett’s famous quote on investing highlights patience, long term wealth building, and the importance of trusting strong businesses over short term market trends.

articles.title: Bitcoin rebounds toward $78,000 on strong Nvidia earnings, steady accumulation
articles.description: Bitcoin surged nearly 1.5% to around $77,934, buoyed by strong Nvidia earnings and steady buyer accumulation, despite ongoing ETF outflows. Ethereum also saw gains, with major altcoins like BNB and Solana rallying significantly. Experts suggest this rebound indicates resilience and selective strength in the crypto market, with institutional interest remaining a key factor.

articles.description: Fuel prices in India are under sharp focus amid concerns of another hike due to volatile global crude markets. This follows a recent Rs 3 per litre increase, the first in four years, linked to Middle East geopolitical tensions.

articles.description: Peak XV Partners is a common investor across all three companies, while Steadview Capital backs both Temple and Ethereal Machines. Zetwerk founders are all angel investors in Ethereal Machines, as reported by Moneycontrol.

articles.title: Gold and silver rates on May 21, 2026: Check prices across key cities
articles.description: Gold prices in India remained largely stable on 21 May 2026, with marginal gains recorded across major purity categories, while silver prices edged slightly lower amid fluctuations in global bullion markets.

articles.title: JSW Motors ties up $826 million funding from India’s top bank
articles.description: JSW Motors, part of Sajjan Jindal's business empire, has secured a significant 80 billion rupee funding line. This financial boost from State Bank of India will fuel the development of a new greenfield manufacturing facility in Maharashtra. The venture aims to establish India's first homegrown new energy passenger vehicle brand in decades.

articles.title: Banks look risky, bet on EVs and innovators: Dipan Mehta on where to put your money now
articles.description: Market expert Dipan Mehta advises investors to focus on individual stocks rather than the Nifty amidst market volatility. He suggests avoiding oil marketing companies and traditional banks, instead favoring EV-focused auto ancillaries, upstream oil producers, and NBFCs. Mehta also highlights innovative companies across sectors as key opportunities for discerning investors.

articles.title: Russia accused of plundering Ukraine's grain, coal and minerals in occupied territories
articles.description: First, Russia took Ukraine's land. Now, Ukrainian officials and legal experts say, Moscow is stripping and selling what lies above and beneath it.

articles.description: Starlink had 10.3 million paid subscribers in Q1, double the 5 million it had a year ago, according to SpaceX's S-1 filing.

articles.description: The crisis has been most acute in rural pockets, where diesel rationing and refusal to sell fuel in cans have slowed pre-monsoon agricultural work, forcing farmers to leave tractors idle for hours.

articles.title: Sensex gains 560 points, Nifty above 23,800. Key factors behind today's Dalal Street rally
articles.description: Indian stock markets surged in early trading, with Sensex and Nifty extending gains for a second day. A stronger rupee, falling bond yields, and positive investor sentiment fueled the rally. Broader markets and most sectoral indices also saw significant upticks, indicating a 'buy on dips' trend.

articles.title: Months after tie-up with OpenAI and Anthropic, Intuit to layoff 3,000 employees; CEO says in memo: Simplifying structure will help ...
articles.description: Financial software giant Intuit is reportedly laying off nearly 17% of its global workforce, impacting around 3,000 employees. This significant restructuring, aimed at simplifying operations and boosting AI integration, follows recent partnerships with OpenAI and Anthropic. The move comes as Intuit, like other tech firms, pivots towards AI-driven efficiencies amidst broader industry layoffs.

articles.title: I-T dept awareness event on new tax laws
articles.description: Income Tax dept held “Prarambh 2026” awareness event at Lucknow University on new Income Tax Act 2025 and Rules 2026, effective April 1, 2026.

articles.title: West Asia conflict fails to slow oil PSUs' capex push
articles.description: Indian oil companies are pushing ahead with major expansion and clean energy projects. Indian Oil Corporation plans significant investments in refining and renewable energy. Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited is also allocating substantial funds for refining and marketing. Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited's capital expenditure is expected to be slightly lower. These companies are focused on growth and energy transition.

articles.title: SpaceX IPO filing lays bare losses and Elon Musk control as it stakes future on AI
articles.description: SpaceX has filed for its IPO, revealing substantial losses in its artificial intelligence division. The company's future hinges on unproven technologies like space data centers. Starlink, its satellite internet service, remains the only profitable segment. The IPO could be the largest ever, valuing SpaceX at a record amount.

articles.title: Lower user development fee okayed for Navi Mumbai Airport
articles.description: Navi Mumbai International Airport will see lower passenger charges. India's airport tariff regulator has approved reduced user development fees. Domestic flyers will pay ₹620 and international flyers ₹1,225. This decision moderates charges at the new airport. The regulator also cut the airport's revenue requirement significantly. These changes will impact revenue recovery for the operator.

articles.title: West Asia crisis hits date trade, prices jump as supplies shrink in Indore
articles.description: West Asia crisis disrupts date imports from UAE, Iran and Iraq, cutting arrivals in Indore and pushing dry fruit prices up 10–30% amid shipping delays.

articles.description: HSBC and Standard Chartered signalled deeper AI-driven restructuring across the banking sector, with executives warning that technology will eliminate some roles while creating new opportunities.

articles.title: Consumer Panel Orders Hotel Mountview Refund
articles.description: The State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission Chandigarh has upheld an order directing Hotel Mountview, operated by Chandigarh Industrial and Tourism Development Corporation Limited (CITCO), to refund a portion of the booking amount paid for a cancelled marriage function. The Commission dismissed CITCO’s appeal against an earlier ruling by the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission, which [...] The post Consumer Panel Orders Hotel Mountview Refund appeared first on Hospitality Biz India: Latest News & Analysis .
articles.source: Hospitality Biz India: Latest News & Analysis

articles.title: Economic storm at home, PM making reels abroad: Opposition
articles.description: Speaking in Rae Bareli, Gandhi said the Prime Minister asked people to use less petrol, not buy gold, spend less and avoid travelling abroad.

articles.title: Delhi's power demand hits record 8,039 MW in 2026 amid heatwave
articles.description: Delhi's peak power demand hit a record 8,039 MW on Wednesday due to rising temperatures, a new high for 2026. This reflects a sharp increase in electricity consumption, consistently surpassing levels from 2025 and 2024 for the same period.

articles.title: Honouring contracts: Cos seek to export 150,000 tonnes sugar
articles.description: India's sugar industry seeks permission for additional exports. This follows a recent ban on overseas shipments. The industry needs to honor existing contracts with international buyers. The government imposed the ban due to concerns over El Nino and rising domestic prices. This move aims to ensure sufficient sugar for local consumption and build buffer stocks for the next year.

articles.title: Where Donald Trump stands with Republicans nationally, according to the latest AP-NORC poll
articles.description: WASHINGTON — Republicans are unhappier with President Donald Trump's handling of the economy than they were a few months ago, but they're largely continuing to stand behind him as the war with Iran continues, a new AP-NORC poll finds. About 6 in 10 Republicans approve of how Trump is handling the ...

articles.title: Axis Pension Fund targets 500K new subscribers and ₹30,000 crore AUM
articles.description: The pension fund manager expects onboarding through BHIM, Google Pay and PhonePe, along with new products, to drive subscriber growth in FY27

articles.title: RBI announces $5 billion USD/INR swap auction on May 26 to inject liquidity
articles.description: RBI liquidity: The Reserve Bank of India will conduct a $5 billion USD-INR buy-sell swap auction on May 26 to infuse long-term liquidity into the banking system. The move comes as the rupee faces pressure amid global uncertainty and market volatility.

articles.title: Govt to roll out VB-G RAM G scheme from July 1; Parliamentary committee briefed
articles.description: The VB-G RAM G, which replaces MGNREGA, guarantees 125 days of wage employment per rural household in each financial year to such households whose adult members volunteer to undertake unskilled manual work, contributing to income security beyond the earlier 100-day entitlement.

articles.title: Warehousing beats all odds amid global supply chaos
articles.description: India's warehousing sector shows strong growth in the first quarter of 2026. Leasing activity increased by 15 percent compared to the previous year, reaching 19.3 million square feet across eight major cities. This surge is fueled by demand from manufacturing and third-party logistics firms. Companies are actively enhancing their supply chain networks and distribution capabilities.
